text:
```json
{
  "activation_status": "activated",
  "risk_level": "elevated",
  "confidence": 0.74,
  "drivers": [
    "RBI executed a 50 bps cut on April 8 2026 bringing repo to 4.75% with stance shifted to Accommodative, compressing risk-free anchor rates and improving relative attractiveness of private credit yield premium over sovereign benchmarks",
    "Real rate derived at 3.15% (repo 4.75% minus core CPI 3.94%) remains materially positive, indicating monetary conditions are not yet loose enough to erode private credit spread advantage, though the direction of travel is downward",
    "10Y G-Sec yield at 6.99% (as of Apr 17 2026), having risen 44 bps from 6.55% in November 2025 and spiked to 7.19% on April 3, signals that the long end is not fully pricing the accommodative pivot; yield curve steepening creates mark-to-market risk for duration-exposed debt instruments but is broadly neutral for private credit AIFs with floating or fixed-coupon structures",
    "Brent crude at $118.95/barrel (Apr 29 2026), up 86% YoY from ~$64, with Hormuz disruption blocking approximately 20% of global oil trade, represents a material stagflationary shock vector for India as a net oil importer; this elevates input cost inflation risk (services PMI input costs at 45-month high in March 2026) and constrains RBI's cutting room",
    "INR depreciated 10.7% over 12 months to 84.2 vs USD (Apr 29 2026) with forex reserves declining $25B to $703B; for a Resident investor deploying into a domestic INR-denominated Cat II AIF this is not a direct currency risk but signals external balance stress that could tighten domestic liquidity conditions",
    "FII equity outflows of Rs 56,401 Cr in April 2026 alone (~$5.97B), partially offset by DII inflows of Rs 34,617 Cr (partial data Apr 1-8), indicate a stressed FII-DII dynamic; this is not directly load-bearing for a private credit AIF but signals risk-off sentiment in Indian capital markets broadly",
    "GDP growth at 6.8% YoY in Q3 FY26 (Oct-Dec 2025) with RBI projecting FY26 at 7.6%; manufacturing at 13.3% and services at 9.5% growth; capacity utilization at 75.6% (above the 75% late-expansion threshold per framework); this growth backdrop supports borrower credit quality in private credit portfolios",
    "Non-food credit growth at 14.3% YoY (Feb 2026, up from 11.1% a year prior) signals healthy underlying credit demand, supportive of deal origination environment for private credit managers",
    "IMD forecasting below-normal monsoon at 92% of LPA with 66% probability of below-normal or deficient outcome (El Nino likely); this is a leading inflation risk signal for food CPI in H2 FY27, which could constrain RBI's ability to cut further and keep long-end yields elevated",
    "Government fiscal consolidation on track: fiscal deficit YTD at 80.38% of RE with primary deficit down 52.5% YoY; capex at Rs 9.29L Cr (84.81% of RE, +14.46% YoY); FY27 target of 4.3% GDP deficit with Rs 12.22L Cr capex; quality of expenditure improving (capex up, revenue expenditure flat); this is a constructive backdrop for infrastructure-linked private credit strategies",
    "SEBI AIF Third Amendment Regulations 2025 (effective November 18 2025) introduced the AIOF framework and LVF unification; AIF Master Circular consolidation (effective May 7 2024) codified pro-rata pari-passu provisions; mandatory dematerialisation of AIF units (effective January 1 2023) is operative; regulatory framework for Cat II AIFs is stable and recently consolidated",
    "Source of funds is cash balance (not fixed deposits), so FD reinvestment compression is not directly applicable; however, the rate-cut cycle does reduce the opportunity cost of deploying idle cash into a longer-duration illiquid instrument, marginally improving the deployment rationale"
  ],
  "flags": [
    "crude_oil_hormuz_shock_stagflationary_risk",
    "inr_depreciation_10pct_12m_external_stress",
    "fii_outflow_risk_off_sentiment_april_2026",
    "below_normal_monsoon_food_inflation_upside_h2_fy27",
    "long_end_yield_volatility_10y_gsec_44bps_rise",
    "real_rate_3pct_plus_rbi_cutting_room_constrained_by_geopolitics",
    "capital_call_schedule_reconciliation_required_sadashivanagar_reserve_and_parents_care_fund",
    "rnor_to_resident_transition_partial_year_tax_treatment_ca_confirmation_pending",
    "macro_snapshot_data_lag_walr_q1_2026_pending_120_days_stale",
    "equity_band_breach_current_6pct_vs_55_70pct_mandate_macro_context_does_not_resolve_rebalancing_priority"
  ],
  "reasoning_paragraph": "The macro environment as of April 2026 presents a bifurcated picture for the proposed Rs 5 Cr commitment to Vivriti Alpha Debt Fund (Cat II private credit AIF). On the constructive side, the RBI's 50 bps cut to 4.75% with an Accommodative stance, against a backdrop of 6.8% real GDP growth, 75.6% capacity utilization, 14.3% non-food credit growth, and a fiscally consolidating government running strong capex, creates a supportive domestic credit environment: borrower quality is underpinned by above-trend growth, deal origination is supported by healthy credit demand, and the rate-cut direction compresses the opportunity cost of illiquid deployment relative to liquid alternatives. The SEBI regulatory framework for Cat II AIFs is stable and recently consolidated through the 2024 Master Circular and 2025 Third Amendment, with no imminent regulatory inflection that would alter the product's structural position. However, three external stress vectors materially elevate the risk overlay. First, Brent crude at $118.95/barrel (up 86% YoY) with active Hormuz disruption represents a genuine stagflationary shock for India: it pressures the current account, has already driven services input cost inflation to a 45-month high, and constrains RBI's ability to cut further, keeping the long end of the yield curve volatile (10Y G-Sec at 6.99%, having spiked to 7.19% on April 3). Second, INR depreciation of 10.7% over 12 months to 84.2 with forex reserves declining $25B signals external balance stress that, while not a direct risk for an INR-denominated domestic AIF, tightens the systemic liquidity backdrop. Third, FII outflows of Rs 56,401 Cr in April alone signal a risk-off posture in Indian capital markets that, while more relevant to equity than private credit, is a leading indicator of broader sentiment deterioration. The below-normal monsoon forecast (92% of LPA, 66% probability of below-normal or deficient) adds a medium-term food inflation upside risk that could further constrain RBI's cutting cycle. Within the case-specific lens, the source of funds is cash balance rather than fixed deposits, so FD reinvestment compression is not the operative mechanism; the relevant framing is that idle cash deployed into an 8-year private credit structure at a time of accommodative monetary policy and above-trend growth captures a yield premium over declining risk-free rates, but does so against a backdrop of elevated external stress. The capital-call schedule reconciliation against the Rs 12-14 Cr Sadashivanagar property reserve and Rs 50 L parents-care fund is a liquidity management matter that sits outside E3's scope but is flagged as a structural constraint the macro environment does not resolve. The overall E3 assessment is cautionary-to-neutral: the domestic credit and growth environment is supportive, but the external shock complex (crude, INR, FII flows) is sufficiently material to warrant elevated risk classification rather than a clean supportive verdict.",
  "data_points_cited": [

    "Forex reserves $703B, down $25B",
    "FII net equity outflows Rs 56,401 Cr April 1-29 2026 (~$5.97B), heaviest day April 2 Rs 19,837 Cr",
    "DII net inflows Rs 34,617 Cr April 1-8 2026 (partial)",
    "GDP growth 6.8% YoY Q3 FY26 (Oct-Dec 2025), manufacturing 13.3%, services 9.5%; RBI FY26 projection 7.6%",
    "Capacity utilization 75.6% (75.5% SA) Q3 FY26, above 75% late-expansion threshold",
    "Non-food credit growth 14.3% YoY as of February 28 2026, up from 11.1% a year prior",
    "CPI headline 4.13% YoY March 2026; core CPI 3.94% YoY Jan-Feb 2026",
    "Services PMI input cost inflation at 45-month high March 2026; PMI Manufacturing 55.1, PMI Services 59.0, Composite PMI 58.3 (April 2026 flash)",
    "IMD monsoon forecast below normal 92% of LPA, 66% probability below-normal or deficient, El Nino likely, April 13 2026",
    "Fiscal deficit YTD Apr-Feb FY26 Rs 12,52,649 Cr (80.38% of RE), down 7.0% YoY; primary deficit down 52.5% YoY",
    "Government capex YTD Rs 9,29,322 Cr (84.81% of RE), up 14.46% YoY",
    "FY27 fiscal deficit target 4.3% GDP, capex Rs 12.22L Cr",
    "WALR on fresh loans 8.28% as of December 2025, down from 9.37% peak, 109 bps transmission",
    "India-US rate differential 162 bps (repo 5.25% minus Fed midpoint 3.625%) — note: snapshot reflects pre-cut repo; post-cut differential narrows further",
    "SEBI AIF Third Amendment Regulations 2025 effective November 18 2025 (AIOF framework, LVF unification)",
    "AIF Master Circular consolidation effective May 7 2024 (pro-rata pari-passu codification)",
    "AIF mandatory dematerialisation effective January 1 2023",
    "SEBI minimum ticket Cat II AIF Rs 100 lakh (sebi_009)"
```
